rationale: Ground truth has zero substantive concerns (just LGTM/approve), so recall is trivially satisfied by both. Both candidates do solid diff-grounded verification of the PR's technical claims with specific code citations; neither fabricates issues. X adds one genuinely actionable, concrete suggestion (stale extra_body pattern in a sibling README with a proposed fix), giving it real actionability, whereas Y's only comment is a hedged non-request ('keep current wording if you tested it') that asks for no concrete change. Y's verification is marginally deeper/cleaner and stays strictly in-diff, edging precision slightly, but X's added value outweighs that on actionability, giving it a slight overall edge.
